scenarios.name: ESB connection pool exhaustion
scenarios.narrative: T24 core service response times slowly creep up (60 → 180ms p50). ESB holds connections longer waiting for T24 responses; connection pool saturates at ~70% utilization. Once pool is full, ESB starts queueing requests; queue depth grows linearly. Eventually p99 crosses 2s threshold and alerts fire on ESB. Datapower (calls ESB) inherits the latency.

log_patterns.pattern: Failed to forward request to t24-service: pool exhausted

expected_rca.explanation: ESB conn_pool_used crossed 70% before any alert fired. T24 latency drift is the upstream trigger, ESB is where saturation happens.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: datapower depends on esb, both degraded; t24 is leaf, only its latency drifted (not error)

discussion_prompts: Tại sao alert fire trên ESB nhưng root cause có thể là T24?
Nếu chỉ nhìn alert feed (không nhìn topology), bạn sẽ remediate sai như thế nào?
Pool saturation tăng linear trong 15' — anomaly detector nào (3σ, IsolationForest, LSTM) catch được sớm nhất?

scenarios.name: DataPower DNS NXDOMAIN burst
scenarios.narrative: K8s cluster's CoreDNS reload picks up a bad upstream config. NXDOMAIN responses spike for `esb.svc.cluster.local`. Datapower (calls ESB by service name) sees DNS resolution fail, retries with exponential backoff, latency explodes. ESB is healthy but unreachable from datapower's POV.

log_patterns.pattern: DNS resolution failed for esb.svc.cluster.local: NXDOMAIN
Retry exhausted after 5 attempts, returning 503 to client

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: rollback coredns configmap to last known good

expected_rca.explanation: ESB metrics show NO degradation — pure infra failure. RCA must look at infra layer, not just app layer.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: ESB perfectly healthy from its own view; datapower → esb edge is the broken hop

discussion_prompts: PageRank trên service topology không tìm được CoreDNS — vì sao? Cần extension nào?
Khi nào nên trust ESB self-report 'healthy' vs Datapower's 'esb unreachable'?
Anti-pattern: nếu remediate bằng cách restart ESB sẽ thế nào?

scenarios.name: ROC python GIL contention spike
scenarios.narrative: Recent deploy of roc-installment-service added a new /score-application endpoint that does heavy Python computation (no asyncio yield). When 5+ concurrent requests hit it, GIL contention starves the asyncio event loop. Other endpoints (healthchecks, simple GETs) start timing out even though CPU isn't pegged.

log_patterns.pattern: asyncio: task took 2.4s to yield, event loop lag detected

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: feature-flag /score-application off; deploy fix moves heavy compute to ThreadPoolExecutor

expected_rca.explanation: CPU không pegged (~80% nhưng có headroom) — confusing signal. GIL wait là smoking gun. Bb-edge degraded vì upstream timeout.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: bb-edge → roc-installment edge degraded; bb-edge self CPU/mem fine

discussion_prompts: Nếu chỉ có CPU + latency, làm sao distinguish GIL contention vs CPU starvation?
Anomaly detection thấy gil_wait drift sớm 15' trước alert — pre-incident forecast có nên trigger preemptive remediation không?

scenarios.name: BB-edge TLS cert expiry
scenarios.narrative: Wildcard TLS cert (*.app.internal) expired at 03:00 UTC. cert-manager autorenew was disabled during a maintenance window 2 weeks ago and never re-enabled. BB-edge fails TLS handshake for ALL incoming requests; error rate jumps to 100% instantly. No upstream/downstream cascade — pure ingress failure.

log_patterns.pattern: TLS handshake failed: certificate has expired (notAfter=2026-06-09T03:00:00Z)

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: manual cert rotation via cert-manager Certificate CR + reload pods

expected_rca.explanation: Cascade KHÔNG xảy ra — bb-edge là entrypoint, downstream (roc-installment, ...) khoẻ vì không có traffic đến nữa. RCA nhìn upstream sẽ bị lừa: 'roc-installment perfectly healthy, must be bb-edge bug'.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: bb-edge isolated failure; downstream RPS drops because input dries up, not because they're broken

discussion_prompts: Pre-incident forecast: tls_cert_days_until_expiry=7 nên trigger ticket không?
Tại sao downstream RPS drop 90% lại là HEALTH SIGN, không phải failure?
Anti-pattern: PagerDuty fire bb-edge AND roc-installment vì cascade — paging fatigue?

scenarios.name: JVM memory leak in ESB
scenarios.narrative: ESB caches T24 responses with no TTL. Memory grows linearly over hours (200 → 850MB). GC pause time increases proportionally. At ~80% heap, full GC pauses hit 4s every 2 min, causing intermittent latency spikes. Eventually OOMKilled and k8s restart.

log_patterns.pattern: OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space at com.example.cache.ResponseCache.put

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: rolling restart + hotfix add 10min TTL to ResponseCache

expected_rca.explanation: Spiky latency every 2 min (GC pauses) là pattern khó cho 3σ — alert miss khi metric oscillate. Memory monotonic growth là smoking gun.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: Datapower spiky latency CORRELATED với ESB GC pauses — Granger causality test confirms

discussion_prompts: 3σ alert miss spiky metric vì mean cao + std cao. LSTM-AE bắt được không?
Memory drift linear 4h trước OOM — forecast model nào dự đoán được time-to-OOM?
Đây là cách bạn explain cho 1 product manager: 'metric oscillate nhưng vẫn là incident'.

scenarios.name: T24 backend timeout cascades up
scenarios.narrative: T24 end-of-day batch job competes with online traffic for DB replicas. T24 latency goes from 80ms p50 → 1200ms p50. ESB calls timeout (3s threshold). Datapower retries 3x then 504s. BB-edge users see 'transaction pending' but no clear error. 4-hop cascade across the entire backend chain.

log_patterns.pattern: Timeout waiting for t24-service response after 3000ms
Retry 3/3 failed for esb endpoint, returning 504

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: scale t24 read replicas 2->4 + throttle batch job to off-hours window

expected_rca.explanation: 4-hop cascade. Naive 'fix the alerting service' = fix wrong thing. PageRank trên reverse topology (downstream-aware) ranks t24 highest dù alerts fire ngược chiều.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: Alerts fire bb-edge → datapower → esb → t24 over 6 minutes. RCA must traverse reverse to find earliest drift.

discussion_prompts: Naive: fix alerting service first. PageRank reverse-topology: fix t24 first. Discuss.
Alert sequence ngược cascade direction — vì sao? (downstream khoẻ bị block trước upstream alert thresh)
Granger causality: db_replica_lag → latency drift, lag=30sec. Đủ evidence cho RCA tự tin?

scenarios.name: bb-confirmation-service OOM silent cascade
scenarios.narrative: bb-confirmation-service pod gets OOMKilled. k8s restarts it but the service has a 'graceful degradation' fallback in roc-installment that returns stale data when confirmation is unreachable. No errors propagate; users get stale confirmation status. Only metrics: bb-confirmation pod restart_count++, roc-installment cache_hit_rate spikes from 5% → 80%.

log_patterns.pattern: Fallback to cached confirmation (age=12min) due to upstream unreachable

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: increase bb-confirmation pod mem limit 512Mi -> 1Gi + alert on stale_response_count > 50/min

expected_rca.explanation: SILENT cascade — no error propagates. Only 1 k8s-event alert (low severity). RCA must look at unusual metric SHIFTS (cache_hit_rate jump), not error counts.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: Latency/error metrics on all services NORMAL. Only proxy signal: cache_hit_rate jumps to 80%.

discussion_prompts: Anomaly detector chỉ alert khi error/latency. Cache_hit_rate jump không tự nó là 'bad' — làm sao detect?
Graceful degradation HIDES incidents. Tradeoff: ổn định user-facing vs visibility cho ops team?
Đây là 'unknown unknown' — alert nào sẽ cover? (Hint: alert on metric SHIFT, không phải absolute threshold)

scenarios.name: Datapower-ESB network partition
scenarios.narrative: DevOps applies new NetworkPolicy to namespace `services` for compliance audit. Policy missing egress rule for esb → datapower. From datapower's POV: ESB is unreachable. From esb's POV: requests stopped coming, looks like quiet period. Bilateral observability is needed.

log_patterns.pattern: connection refused: dial tcp 10.42.3.12:8080: i/o timeout (esb endpoint)

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: rollback NetworkPolicy via kubectl + add ingress rule for datapower SA

expected_rca.explanation: Infra change, not app code. RCA must correlate with k8s audit log (deploy events). Single-service-view sẽ miss — ESB nhìn bình thường (chỉ ít traffic hơn).
expected_rca.topology_evidence: Asymmetric signal: datapower tcp_connect_failure_count high, esb rps low. Combined = network issue between them.

discussion_prompts: ESB rps drop 60% — looks healthy or unhealthy? Cần baseline.
Auto-remediation: rollback last k8s apply 5' trước incident. An toàn không? (rollback policy = mở firewall = compliance violation)
Closed-loop decision: nếu RCA confidence < 0.7 thì page on-call thay vì auto-act. Threshold thực tế?

scenarios.name: Chaos-daemon resource hog noisy neighbor
scenarios.narrative: Someone runs `kubectl apply -f chaos-experiment.yaml` with `cpu_load: '100'` instead of `'10'` (typo). Chaos-daemon DaemonSet eats 100% CPU on every node. Co-located pods (roc-installment, esb) get CPU throttled. Latency spikes correlate exactly with chaos-daemon CPU spike.

log_patterns.pattern: chaos-experiment cpu-burn started: load=100, duration=900s, pods=all

expected_rca.explanation: Multiple alerting services (roc, esb) SHARE node với chaos-daemon. Topology không kết nối chaos-daemon → roc trong code, nhưng SHARES infra. RCA cần node-level view, không chỉ service-level.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: Service graph KHÔNG cho thấy chaos-daemon là root (no edges). Cần node-affinity metric.

discussion_prompts: Service graph PageRank fail ở đây. Cần augment với node-level metrics (per-node CPU/mem). Như thế nào?
Auto-remediation: kill chaos-experiment vs cordon node? Tradeoffs.
Pre-incident forecast trên chaos-daemon CPU drift 15' trước alert: action?

scenarios.name: Slow-burn ML drift — forecast catches before threshold
scenarios.narrative: ML model in roc-installment scores loan applications. Training distribution doesn't match weekend application pattern (more refinance, less new). Model latency creeps up (40 → 110ms) over 4 hours — never crosses static p99 threshold but pre-incident forecast model flags 'p99 will cross 2s in 90min' with 78% confidence. Demo of proactive RCA flow.

alerts.note: predictive alert from L2 forecasting engine

log_patterns.pattern: Feature drift detected: weekend_indicator distribution shift 0.78

phases.name: recovery
phases.remediation: shadow-deploy retrained model (trained on 4-week rolling window) + canary 10% traffic

expected_rca.explanation: KHÔNG có alert nào fire (chưa cross threshold). Pre-incident forecast là signal duy nhất. RCA pivot: instead of explaining current state, RCA explains predicted state.
expected_rca.topology_evidence: Single service, no cascade. RCA is feature drift correlation, not topology.

discussion_prompts: Pre-incident forecast confidence 78%. Auto-remediate hay page human? Production threshold?
Shadow deploy của retrained model = remediation. Nhưng nếu retrained model TỆ hơn thì sao? Rollback strategy.
Đây là demo của closed-loop FULL: detect → predict → enrich → decide → act → verify. Liệt kê các phase tương ứng.
